name: Aristaeus
description: Aristaeus was a minor but important deity in Greek mythology, revered as the protector and benefactor of various rural pursuits. He was the son of Apollo and the nymph Cyrene, making him a figure of both divine power and earthly grace. Aristaeus was credited with discovering and teaching humanity essential skills related to agriculture, animal husbandry, and beekeeping. He was believed to have taught humans how to cultivate olives, make cheese, and manage beehives, bringing prosperity and sustenance to communities. His influence extended to viticulture, the art of wine-making, and he was often associated with the flourishing of orchards and fields. Aristaeus's journey was marked by both success and tragedy. He was said to have pursued Eurydice, the wife of Orpheus, leading to her accidental death and the subsequent devastation of Orpheus. This event led to plagues and famine, which Aristaeus sought to remedy by consulting Proteus, a shape-shifting sea god, who instructed him to appease the spirits of the dead. Aristaeus's story highlights the importance of respecting the natural world and the interconnectedness of life. He is a symbol of agricultural innovation, the taming of nature for human benefit, and the responsibility that comes with such power. His worship was particularly prominent in regions known for their agricultural output, reflecting the dependence of ancient Greek society on the bounty of the land and the skills that ensured its abundance. He is often depicted as a handsome young man, accompanied by symbols of his domains, such as bees, olive branches, and agricultural tools.
subtitle: God of Beekeeping, Agriculture, and Husbandry

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Aristaeus
keyMyths.description: Aristaeus was born to Apollo and Cyrene, a nymph. Apollo gifted him with knowledge of various rural arts.

keyMyths.title: Aristaeus and Eurydice
keyMyths.description: Aristaeus pursued Eurydice, leading to her death and Orpheus's grief. This event caused a plague and famine.

keyMyths.title: Consulting Proteus
keyMyths.description: To end the plague, Aristaeus consulted Proteus. Proteus revealed the cause and instructed Aristaeus to appease the spirits.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: A didactic poem on agriculture, including the story of Aristaeus.
sources.text: A didactic poem on agriculture, including the story of Aristaeus.

sources.author: Diodorus Siculus
sources.description: Provides a comprehensive account of Greek mythology, including Aristaeus's role in agriculture.
sources.text: Provides a comprehensive account of Greek mythology, including Aristaeus's role in agriculture.
